stories.title: Kitzbühel Semifinals Day — Bublik/Etcheverry, Hanfmann/Halys; QF Score Correction
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP (July 23 pipeline) + CORRECTION — The Generali Open Kitzbühel (ATP 250) reaches its semifinals Friday. CORRECTION: Yesterday's pipeline incorrectly reported Navone defeated Halys 6-0, 6-3 in the quarterfinals. The actual result was Halys defeating Navone 7-5, 6-3. Also correcting other QF scores: Bublik def. Molcan 6-3, 6-2; Etcheverry def. Buse 6-2, 7-5; Hanfmann def. Baez 6-3, 6-1. SF pairings: (1) Bublik vs. Etcheverry; (2) Hanfmann vs. Halys. H2H: Halys leads Hanfmann 2-0; Bublik leads Etcheverry 2-0. Hanfmann is the oldest player to reach a Kitzbühel Open Era semifinal at 34 years, 8 months. Bublik is chasing back-to-back titles — the last player to successfully defend Kitzbühel did so in 2013. SF results not yet indexed at pipeline run time.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Correction + Kitzbühel SFs 🎾

Yesterday we had the wrong QF result: Halys beat Navone 7-5, 6-3 — not the reverse. Apologies.

SFs today:
▸ Bublik vs. Etcheverry
▸ Hanfmann vs. Halys

Final is Saturday.

#ATPKitzbuhel #GeneraliOpen

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Yannick Hanfmann is 34 years old and just reached his FOURTH Kitzbühel semifinal — the most of any player in the Open Era.

He faces Quentin Halys today. The crowd in Austria knows this name well.

#ATPKitzbuhel #GeneraliOpen

facebook.long_form:
We owe you a correction before we preview today's semifinals at Kitzbühel.

In yesterday's coverage, we reported that Mariano Navone defeated Quentin Halys 6-0, 6-3 in the quarterfinals. That was wrong. The actual result was Halys defeating Navone 7-5, 6-3. We also had slightly off scores on a couple of other QFs: Bublik beat Molcan 6-3, 6-2 (not 6-3, 7-5), Etcheverry beat Buse 6-2, 7-5 (not 7-6(4)), and Hanfmann beat Baez 6-3, 6-1. Apologies for the errors — here are the corrected records.

Because Halys beat Navone, the SF bracket is: Bublik vs. Etcheverry, and Hanfmann vs. Halys (not Hanfmann vs. Navone as we stated).

On the tennis itself: Yannick Hanfmann is an unusual protagonist for a semifinal story. The German is 34 years old and today marks his fourth Kitzbühel semifinal in the Open Era — more than any other player in this event's history. That kind of connection to one tournament over a long career is a real thing in tennis, and Kitzbühel has clearly been a happy hunting ground for Hanfmann.

Meanwhile, Bublik — the defending champion — is trying to go back-to-back. The last player to successfully defend the Kitzbühel title did so in 2013.

Who do you think wins the final on Saturday?

---

stories.title: Estoril QF Day — Rublev "Most Dominant Win of 2026," All Four QFs Set
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP (July 22/23 pipeline) — Top seed Andrey Rublev (RUS, No. 14) demolished Timofey Skatov 6-1, 6-1 in 59 minutes Thursday — ATP Tour called it his "most dominant win of 2026." The Bastad champion now meets Van Assche (FRA) in the Millennium Estoril Open (ATP 250) quarterfinals Friday. Full QF slate: Rublev vs. Van Assche; Burruchaga (ARG) vs. Blockx (BEL, No. 4 seed); Darderi (ITA, No. 2) vs. Faria (POR, Portuguese wildcard/crowd favorite); Torres (ESP, wildcard) vs. Gaston (FRA). Torres upset No. 3 seed Tabilo 6-4, 6-4 in R2 to reach the QF. Darderi beat Martinez 6-0, 6-1. Faria beat Portuguese compatriot Bueno 6-2, 6-4. SFs scheduled Saturday.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Rublev demolished Skatov 6-1, 6-1 in 59 minutes at Estoril on Thursday.

ATP Tour called it his most dominant win of 2026. Fresh off the Bastad title.

QFs today: Rublev/Van Assche + Darderi/Faria + Torres/Gaston + Burruchaga/Blockx.

#EstorilOpen

facebook.long_form:
Andrey Rublev arrived in Estoril coming off a Bastad title last weekend — and on Thursday he wasted absolutely no time proving he belongs at the top of the draw.

Rublev defeated Timofey Skatov 6-1, 6-1 in 59 minutes. The ATP Tour itself described that performance as Rublev's most dominant win of 2026. On a surface where close sets and tactical battles are the norm, dropping just two games in 59 minutes is a statement.

Today he faces Luca Van Assche in the quarterfinals at the Millennium Estoril Open. The rest of the QF bracket is equally compelling: Luciano Darderi — who faced Rublev in the Bastad final — takes on Jaime Faria, a Portuguese wildcard who will have the Lisbon crowd firmly in his corner. Torres (another wildcard, who upset No. 3 seed Tabilo 6-4, 6-4 in R2) faces Gaston. And Roman Andres Burruchaga — the Argentine who beat Wawrinka in his final clay match at this tournament — takes on fourth seed Alexander Blockx.

Four quarterfinals. Saturday semifinals. Can Rublev carry his Bastad form all the way to a second straight title?

Which QF are you most excited about today?

---

stories.title: WTA Prague QF Day — Krejcikova Beats Havlickova, Bouzkova Advances
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP (July 23 pipeline) — Barbora Krejcikova (CZE) defeated local qualifier Lucie Havlickova (CZE) 6-4, 6-2 in R2 Thursday, extending her winning streak from Athens to seven matches. She now faces Marta Salkova (CZE) in Friday's QF (6:30 PM local; result not yet indexed). In a completed QF, defending champion Marie Bouzkova (CZE) defeated fifth seed Tereza Valentova (CZE) 7-5, 6-4 to reach the semifinals. The Livesport Prague Open (WTA 250, July 20-26) is running as an all-Czech showcase this week.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Barbora Krejcikova beat local qualifier Havlickova 6-4, 6-2 in Prague — extending her Athens winning streak to 7 matches.

QF today vs. Salkova. Defending champ Bouzkova already through to the semis.

All-Czech, all week long. 🎾

#WTAPrague #LivesportPragueOpen

facebook.long_form:
Barbora Krejcikova's run through Prague is starting to feel like something special.

She claimed the Athens title last weekend — her ninth career WTA crown. She then returned home to the Livesport Prague Open, where she beat local qualifier Lucie Havlickova 6-4, 6-2 on Thursday to move into the quarterfinals. That's seven matches in a row, and today she faces Marta Salkova (who beat Dodin 6-1, 6-1 in R2) in a QF that keeps the all-Czech theme going.

Meanwhile, defending champion Marie Bouzkova has already punched her ticket to the semifinals. Bouzkova defeated fifth seed Tereza Valentova 7-5, 6-4 in today's early QF — and as the defending champion in this event, she'll be the one to beat in the semis.

If Krejcikova wins today, we could be looking at a defending champion vs. the hottest player in tennis right now meeting in the Prague final. Czech tennis fans this week are being treated to something rare.

Who are you rooting for in today's Prague QF?

---

stories.title: WTA Hamburg QF Results — Sherif Beats Badosa Again; Oliynykova Holds
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP (July 22 pipeline) — Full Hamburg quarterfinal results from the MSC Hamburg Ladies Open (WTA 250): Mayar Sherif (EGY) defeated Paula Badosa (ESP) 6-2, 6-3 — a rematch of last Sunday's Iasi final where Sherif won via Badosa's retirement. Sherif wins the direct contest on clay too. Oleksandra Oliynykova (UKR, No. 1 seed) defeated Elina Avanesyan (ARM) 6-3, 2-6, 6-2. Anna Bondar (HUN) defeated Alina Charaeva 3-6, 7-5, 6-1. Anhelina Kalinina (UKR) defeated Tamara Korpatsch (GER, home crowd favorite) 6-1, 6-3. Semifinals Saturday: Sherif vs. Kalinina; Bondar vs. Oliynykova.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Mayar Sherif beat Paula Badosa AGAIN — 6-2, 6-3 at Hamburg.

Five days ago Sherif won Iasi when Badosa retired trailing 6-4, 4-0.

Today: the direct match. Same result.

SF: Sherif vs. Kalinina. Bondar vs. Oliynykova.

#WTAHamburg #MSCHamburg

facebook.long_form:
Five days ago, Mayar Sherif won the Iasi title when Paula Badosa retired from the final, trailing 6-4, 4-0. Today they met on the clay in Hamburg — and Sherif did it again.

The Egyptian beat Badosa 6-2, 6-3 in the Hamburg quarterfinal, ending the Spanish player's run and booking her spot in Saturday's semifinal. This time there was no retirement, just a straight defeat. Sherif has now beaten Badosa twice in five days on clay.

Elsewhere in the quarterfinals: No. 1 seed Oleksandra Oliynykova survived a scare from Elina Avanesyan (who had beaten No. 7 seed Putintseva in R2) to win 6-3, 2-6, 6-2. Anna Bondar beat Alina Charaeva 3-6, 7-5, 6-1. And Anhelina Kalinina beat local German hope Tamara Korpatsch 6-1, 6-3.

Saturday's semifinals: Sherif vs. Kalinina, and Bondar vs. Oliynykova.

After beating Badosa twice in five days, do you think Mayar Sherif is on her way to a second straight WTA title?

---

stories.title: Canadian Open Withdrawal List Grows — Andreescu Out, Mboko Out for Season
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP (July 23 pipeline) — New additions to the National Bank Open withdrawal list. Bianca Andreescu (CAN) has confirmed she is withdrawing, citing injury ("it definitely sucks"). Victoria Mboko (CAN, No. 12, defending Toronto women's champion) is confirmed out for the remainder of the season after her knee injury at Queen's Club. Yastremska (UKR) is also confirmed out of the Toronto draw (replaced by Zarazua). The women's field now missing at least nine significant players: Muchova, Mboko, Paolini, Raducanu, Yastremska, Andreescu among them. Draws expected July 29-31. Tournament: women Toronto Aug. 1; men Montreal Aug. 4.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Canadian Open update: Andreescu (home country) is now out. Mboko (defending women's champion) is out for the rest of the 2026 season entirely.

9+ confirmed withdrawals. Draw drops July 31. Tournament Aug. 1 (women) / Aug. 4 (men).

#NationalBankOpen #CanadianOpen

facebook.long_form:
The Canadian Open keeps losing players before it even starts.

Two more significant names joined the withdrawal list today. Bianca Andreescu, who would have been playing in front of home fans in Toronto, confirmed she is out — "it definitely sucks," she said. And Victoria Mboko, the defending Toronto women's champion, has not just withdrawn from the Canadian Open — she is confirmed out for the remainder of the 2026 season entirely, due to the knee injury she sustained at Queen's Club.

Add those to the list already compiled: Karolina Muchova (minor surgery post-Wimbledon), Jasmine Paolini (foot), Emma Raducanu (stress fracture), Dayana Yastremska — on the women's side; and Sinner, Djokovic, Alcaraz, and Draper on the men's side.

That's roughly ten significant players missing from a tournament that doesn't even release its draw until July 31. The August 1 women's event in Toronto and the August 4 men's event in Montreal are going to look very different from what anyone expected.

With so many big names out, which players do you think are set up for a surprise run at the Canadian Open?

---

research.facts:
Canadian Open — More Withdrawals
**New additions since July 23 pipeline (HIGH — multiple sources):**
- Bianca Andreescu (CAN) — confirmed out; quoted "it definitely sucks"
- Victoria Mboko (CAN, No. 12) — confirmed out for remainder of 2026 season (not just Canadian Open)
- Yastremska confirmed (mentioned in first research wave; replaced by Zarazua)
**Full confirmed withdrawal list (HIGH — Tennis365, Yardbarker, Tennis Tonic, tennisuptodate):**
Men (Montreal):
- Jannik Sinner (WR No. 1) — elbow; targeting Cincinnati + USO
- Novak Djokovic — groin; going straight to USO
- Carlos Alcaraz (No. 3) — wrist (out since April 14 Barcelona)
- Jack Draper — arm
Women (Toronto):
- Karolina Muchova (No. 6) — minor surgery post-Wimbledon final run
- Victoria Mboko (No. 12, defending champion) — knee injury from fall at Queen's Club; out for rest of 2026 season
- Jasmine Paolini (No. 15) — foot injury; also out of Washington
- Emma Raducanu — stress fracture in leg
- Dayana Yastremska — withdrawn; replaced by Renata Zarazua
- Bianca Andreescu (CAN) — confirmed withdrawal
**Mboko as defending champion (HIGH):** Victoria Mboko won the Toronto women's title in 2025; she will not defend due to knee injury
**Draw release date (HIGH):** July 29-31 (varying source framing; July 31 used in official communications)
**Tournament dates:** Women Toronto Aug. 1; Men Montreal Aug. 4
**Sources:** Tennis365 (8 stars out article), Yardbarker (10+ withdrawals framing), Tennis Tonic (main withdrawal roundup), tennisuptodate (Paolini withdrawal), Canadian Open official communications
